questions.question: Given nums = [10, 20, 30, 40], what is nums[1:3]?
questions.feedback: Slicing is start:stop with stop excluded, so indexes 1 and 2: [20, 30].

questions.question: You have data where each value has a label (name, age, city). Which structure fits best?
questions.feedback: A dict maps labels (keys) to values, exactly this shape of data.

questions.question: What is the result of set([1, 1, 2, 3, 3])?
questions.feedback: A set keeps only unique values, so duplicates are dropped.

questions.question: Which statements are true about strings?

answers.text: They are immutable, methods return a new string
You can slice them like lists, e.g. word[0:3]
name[0] = "X" changes the first letter in place

questions.feedback: Strings are immutable, so you can't assign to an index; the other three are correct.
